mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated FEDFUNDS rate signals continued tight monetary conditions that keep discount rates high, compressing equity valuations and producing muted or negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days relative to periods of falling rates.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or high-FEDFUNDS periods show statistically indistinguishable returns from low-FEDFUNDS periods (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in UNRATE above a 0.2pp threshold signals deteriorating labor conditions that trigger rapid risk-off repricing in equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as markets react quickly to labor deterioration surprises.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following UNRATE increases > 0.2pp are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) across at least 3 distinct calendar years, or hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) signals broad-based consumer and housing demand strength that compounds into sustained corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; neither series alone captures this durable demand-cycle signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST acceleration signal produces 60d forward returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p>0.05), or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime characterized by low unemployment, a deceleration in INDPRO growth signals approaching capacity constraints and margin pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price in slowing industrial output ahead of earnings disappointments.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth turns negative while UNRATE below 5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration signals under low-UNRATE regime fail to produce negative forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window restricted to late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS crosses above a restrictive threshold of 4.5%, cumulative tightening effects on credit availability and corporate financing costs begin to materially compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days that exceed the effect observed at lower rate levels.
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns conditional on FEDFUNDS>4.5% are not significantly different from returns conditional on FEDFUNDS<=4.5% (p>0.05) across at least two distinct tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects ongoing labor market expansion that supports consumer income and spending, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as a continuously active signal independent of specific thresholds.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and forward 5d returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-expansion regime where GDPC1 growth has already decelerated over the prior two quarters, further deceleration in real GDP growth signals an approaching cyclical peak and is followed by negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity risk premia rise ahead of recession probability increases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: Two consecutive quarters of GDPC1 growth deceleration are not followed by negative 60d forward returns in a majority of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 conditional on this regime.

mechanism_narrative: The joint state of elevated FEDFUNDS combined with low UNRATE (a Taylor-rule tightness proxy) signals a policy stance that is restrictive relative to labor market slack, and this combination predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days more strongly than either series alone because it captures the policy-versus-labor-slack imbalance.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with tight labor market
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-UNRATE tightness signal shows no incremental predictive power over either series individually (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10) or forward 20d returns under the joint condition are not significantly negative.
